---
title: Proteome Quality Assessment
subtitle: SpringBloom2021 - Genome-resolved Metaproteomic Dataset
date: 09.04.2025
author: Cyrill Hofer
output:
  rmdformats::readthedown:
    highlight: tango
    self_contained: true
    code_folding: hide
    thumbnails: true
    gallery: false
    fig_width: 15
    df_print: kable
    fig_caption: yes
    use_bookdown: true
    css: custom.css
---

# Data Preparation
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
# Clear R's Brain
rm(list = ls())

# Set the working directory
setwd("/SynologyDrive/PhD/Projects/SpringBloom2021/DataAnalysis_SpringBloom2021/Supplementary/SpringBloom_Metaproteomics/ProteinQualityAssessment/")

# Load the libraries
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)
library(plotly)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(forcats)
library(patchwork)
library(broom)
library(purrr)
library(rstatix) 


# Load the color palette
source("ColorPalette.R")

# Load the data
dat <- read_csv(file = "ProteomeQualityAssessment.csv")
```

# Distribution of raw signal
```{r}
# ============================================================
# Combined density plots of peptide and protein signals
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# This script creates ONE compact figure with:
#   - top row:    peptides
#   - bottom row: proteins
#   - left:       raw signal distribution
#   - right:      log2-transformed signal distribution
#
# Each panel has its own independent x and y scales.
# The figure has:
#   - one shared legend on the RIGHT
#   - one visual global y-axis title on the left
#   - one visual global x-axis title on the bottom row
#
# Density curves are used instead of histogram outlines because
# they are usually cleaner for comparing many samples at once.
#
# Required input:
#   dat
# with at least these columns:
#   - Date
#   - Peptide_ID
#   - Peptide_Abundance
#   - Protein_ID
#   - Protein_Abundance_IBAQ
#
# Optional:
#   ColorPalette
# If ColorPalette does not exist, ggplot default colours are used.
# ============================================================

# ---------------------------
# Load required packages
# ---------------------------
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# ---------------------------
# Basic checks
# ---------------------------
required_cols <- c(
  "Date",
  "Peptide_ID",
  "Peptide_Abundance",
  "Protein_ID",
  "Protein_Abundance_IBAQ"
)

missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, colnames(dat))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop(
    paste0(
      "The following required columns are missing from 'dat': ",
      paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")
    )
  )
}

# ---------------------------
# Prepare peptide data
# ---------------------------
# Keep only positive, non-missing peptide abundances
peptide_dat <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Peptide_Abundance), Peptide_Abundance > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    Date = factor(Date),
    Peptide_Abundance_Log2 = log2(Peptide_Abundance)
  )

# Count distinct peptides for subtitle
peptide_count <- peptide_dat %>%
  summarise(n = n_distinct(Peptide_ID)) %>%
  pull(n)

# ---------------------------
# Prepare protein data
# ---------------------------
# Keep only positive, non-missing protein abundances
protein_dat <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ), Protein_Abundance_IBAQ > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    Date = factor(Date),
    Protein_Abundance_Log2 = log2(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ)
  )

# Count distinct proteins for subtitle
protein_count <- protein_dat %>%
  summarise(n = n_distinct(Protein_ID)) %>%
  pull(n)

# ---------------------------
# Define a common Date factor
# ---------------------------
# This ensures consistent legend order and colour mapping
all_dates <- union(levels(peptide_dat$Date), levels(protein_dat$Date))
peptide_dat$Date <- factor(peptide_dat$Date, levels = all_dates)
protein_dat$Date <- factor(protein_dat$Date, levels = all_dates)

# ---------------------------
# Optional colour handling
# ---------------------------
use_manual_colours <- exists("ColorPalette") &&
  length(ColorPalette) >= length(all_dates)

# ---------------------------
# Common theme for all panels
# ---------------------------
common_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.title.x     = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title       = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position  = "right",
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# ---------------------------
# Helper function to build a density plot
# ---------------------------
make_density_plot <- function(data, xvar, title_text, subtitle_text,
                              xlab = NULL, ylab = "Density") {
  p <- ggplot(
    data,
    aes(x = .data[[xvar]], colour = Date, group = Date)
  ) +
    geom_density(
      linewidth = 0.6,
      alpha = 0.9,
      adjust = 1
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      subtitle = subtitle_text,
      x = xlab,
      y = ylab,
      colour = "Sample / Date"
    ) +
    common_theme
  
  if (use_manual_colours) {
    p <- p + scale_colour_manual(values = ColorPalette)
  }
  
  p
}

# ---------------------------
# Plot 1: Peptides, raw signal
# ---------------------------
Plot_Peptide_Raw <- make_density_plot(
  data = peptide_dat,
  xvar = "Peptide_Abundance",
  title_text = "Peptides",
  subtitle_text = "Raw signal",
  xlab = NULL,
  ylab = "Density"
)

# ---------------------------
# Plot 2: Peptides, log2 signal
# ---------------------------
Plot_Peptide_Log2 <- make_density_plot(
  data = peptide_dat,
  xvar = "Peptide_Abundance_Log2",
  title_text = "Peptides",
  subtitle_text = "Log2-transformed signal",
  xlab = NULL,
  ylab = NULL
)

# ---------------------------
# Plot 3: Proteins, raw signal
# ---------------------------
Plot_Protein_Raw <- make_density_plot(
  data = protein_dat,
  xvar = "Protein_Abundance_IBAQ",
  title_text = "Proteins",
  subtitle_text = "Raw signal",
  xlab = "Signal",
  ylab = "Density"
)

# ---------------------------
# Plot 4: Proteins, log2 signal
# ---------------------------
Plot_Protein_Log2 <- make_density_plot(
  data = protein_dat,
  xvar = "Protein_Abundance_Log2",
  title_text = "Proteins",
  subtitle_text = "Log2-transformed signal",
  xlab = "Signal",
  ylab = NULL
)

# ---------------------------
# Combine the four panels
# ---------------------------
# patchwork keeps each subplot independent while collecting
# the legend into one shared legend on the right.
Plot_Signal_Distribution_Combined <-
  (Plot_Peptide_Raw | Plot_Peptide_Log2) /
  (Plot_Protein_Raw | Plot_Protein_Log2) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Raw and Log2-Transformed Signal Distributions",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Database: Custom | Distinct peptides: ", peptide_count,
      " | Distinct proteins: ", protein_count
    ),
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10)
    )
  ) &
  theme(
    legend.position = "right"
  )

# ---------------------------
# Print the figure
# ---------------------------
Plot_Signal_Distribution_Combined

# ---------------------------
# Save the figure
# ---------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = "Plot_Signal_Distribution_Density_Combined.png",
  plot     = Plot_Signal_Distribution_Combined,
  width    = 16,
  height   = 8,
  units    = "in",
  dpi      = 300
)
```



```{r}
# prepare the data
plot_dat <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Peptide_Abundance & Peptide_Abundance > 0))

# compute the number of unique peptides
peptide_count <- plot_dat %>% 
  summarize(n = n_distinct(Peptide_ID)) %>% 
  pull(n)

# build the plot
Plot_Peptide_Abundance_Histogram <- ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = Peptide_Abundance)) +
  geom_histogram(bins  = 50, fill  = ColorPalette[1], color = "black", linewidth = 0.1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Date, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Peptide Abundance",
       y = "Count",
       title = "Peptide Abundance by Date",
       subtitle = paste0("Database: Custom", "Distinct peptides: ", peptide_count)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 12),
        plot.title      = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10))

# Print it
Plot_Peptide_Abundance_Histogram

# prepare the data
plot_dat <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Peptide_Abundance & Peptide_Abundance > 0)) %>%
  mutate(Peptide_Abundance_Transformed = log2(Peptide_Abundance))

# compute the number of unique peptides
peptide_count <- plot_dat %>% 
  summarize(n = n_distinct(Peptide_ID)) %>% 
  pull(n)

# compute the overall mean of the log2‑transformed abundance
mean_abund <- mean(plot_dat$Peptide_Abundance_Transformed, na.rm = TRUE)

# build the plot with mean line and caption
Plot_Peptide_Abundance_Histogram_Transformed <- ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = Peptide_Abundance_Transformed)) +
  geom_histogram(bins  = 50, fill  = ColorPalette[1], color = "black", linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_abund, color = ColorPalette[4], linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Date, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Peptide Abundance (log2)",
       y = "Count",
       title = "Log2 Transformed Peptide Abundance by Date",
       subtitle = paste0("Database: Custom · Distinct peptides: ", peptide_count),
       caption  = paste0("Overall mean (log2 abundance): ", round(mean_abund, 2))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title      = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.caption    = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 1))

# Print it
Plot_Peptide_Abundance_Combined <- Plot_Peptide_Abundance_Histogram | Plot_Peptide_Abundance_Histogram_Transformed

# Save as A4 PDFs
ggsave("Plot_Peptide_Signal.png",
       plot   = Plot_Peptide_Abundance_Combined,
       width  =  16, height = 9, units = "in", device = "png")
```

# Distribution of protein abundance per sample
```{r}
# prepare the data
plot_dat <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ & Protein_Abundance_IBAQ > 0))

# compute the number of unique proteins
protein_count <- plot_dat %>% 
  summarize(n = n_distinct(Protein_ID)) %>% 
  pull(n)

# build the plot
Plot_Protein_Abundance_Histogram <- ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = Protein_Abundance_IBAQ)) +
  geom_histogram(bins  = 50, fill  = ColorPalette[1], color = "black", linewidth = 0.1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Date, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Protein Abundance",
       y = "Count",
       title = "Protein Abundance by Date",
       subtitle = paste0("Database: Custom", "Distinct proteins: ", protein_count)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 12),
        plot.title      = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10))

# prepare the data
plot_dat <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ & Protein_Abundance_IBAQ > 0)) %>%
  mutate(Protein_Abundance_Transformed = log2(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ))

# compute the number of unique proteins
protein_count <- plot_dat %>% 
  summarize(n = n_distinct(Protein_ID)) %>% 
  pull(n)

# compute the overall mean of the log2‑transformed abundance
mean_abund <- mean(plot_dat$Protein_Abundance_Transformed, na.rm = TRUE)

# build the plot with mean line and caption
Plot_Protein_Abundance_Histogram_Transformed <- ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = Protein_Abundance_Transformed)) +
  geom_histogram(bins  = 50,fill  = ColorPalette[1], color = "black", linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_abund, color = ColorPalette[4], linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Date, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Protein Abundance (log2(IBAQ))",
       y = "Count",
       title = "Log2 Transformed Protein Abundance by Date",
       subtitle = paste0("Database: Custom · Distinct proteins: ", protein_count),
       caption  = paste0("Overall mean (log2 abundance): ", round(mean_abund, 2))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
        axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title      = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.caption    = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 1))


Plot_Protein_Signal <- Plot_Protein_Abundance_Histogram + Plot_Protein_Abundance_Histogram_Transformed

# Save as A4 PDFs
ggsave("Plot_Protein_Signal.png",
       plot   = Plot_Protein_Signal,
       width  =  16, height = 9, units = "in", device = "png")
```

# check the variability of the detected peptides abundance accross samples to visualize eventual batch-effects
```{r}
# prepare the data
plot_dat <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Peptide_Abundance & Peptide_Abundance > 0)) %>%
  mutate(Protein_Abundance_Transformed = log2(Peptide_Abundance))

# Compute your global counts
protein_count <- plot_dat %>% summarize(n = n_distinct(Peptide_ID)) %>% pull(n)
subtitle  <- paste0("Distinct peptides: ", peptide_count)

# Prepare the three data‐frames
df_baseline <- plot_dat %>% 
  mutate(Abundance = Peptide_Abundance)

df_log2 <- plot_dat %>% 
  mutate(Norm_Abundance = log2(Peptide_Abundance))

df_median   <- plot_dat %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  mutate(Median_Log2_Abundance = median(log2(Peptide_Abundance), na.rm = TRUE),
         Centered_Abundance = log2(Peptide_Abundance) - Median_Log2_Abundance) %>%
  ungroup()

# Build each panel
p1 <- ggplot(df_baseline, aes(x = factor(Date), y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Raw Abundance", y = "Peptide Abundance") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

p2 <- ggplot(df_log2, aes(x = factor(Date), y = Norm_Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Log2 Transformed Abundance", y = "Peptide Abundance [log2]") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

p3 <- ggplot(df_median, aes(x = factor(Date), y = Centered_Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Median Centered Log2 Transformed Abundance",
       y = "Peptide Abundance [log2]",
       x = "Date") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

# --- 4) Stack them and add a single title --------------------------------
Plot_Peptide_Abundance_Boxplot_Per_Date <- (p1 / p2 / p3) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Peptide Abundance of deteceted Peptides per Date", subtitle = subtitle,theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
                                                                                                                plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5)))

# Save as A4 PDFs
ggsave("Plot_Peptide_Signal_Overview.png",
       plot   = Plot_Peptide_Abundance_Boxplot_Per_Date,
       width  = 11.69, 
       height =  8.27, 
       units = "in", 
       device = "png")

# prepare the data
plot_dat <- dat %>%
  filter(!is.na(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ & Protein_Abundance_IBAQ > 0)) %>%
  mutate(Protein_Abundance_Transformed = log2(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ))

# Compute your global counts
protein_count <- plot_dat %>% summarize(n = n_distinct(Protein_ID)) %>% pull(n)
subtitle  <- paste0("Distinct proteins: ", protein_count)

# Prepare the three data‐frames
df_baseline <- plot_dat %>% 
  mutate(Abundance = Protein_Abundance_IBAQ)

df_log2 <- plot_dat %>% 
  mutate(Norm_Abundance = log2(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ))

df_median   <- plot_dat %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  mutate(Median_Log2_Abundance = median(log2(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ), na.rm = TRUE),
         Centered_Abundance = log2(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ) - Median_Log2_Abundance) %>%
  ungroup()

# Build each panel
p1 <- ggplot(df_baseline, aes(x = factor(Date), y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Raw Abundance", y = "Protein Abundance [IBAQ]") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

p2 <- ggplot(df_log2, aes(x = factor(Date), y = Norm_Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Log2 Transformed Abundance", y = "Protein Abundance [log2(IBAQ)]") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

p3 <- ggplot(df_median, aes(x = factor(Date), y = Centered_Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Median Centered Log2 Transformed Abundance",
       y = "Protein Abundance [log2(IBAQ]",
       x = "Date") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

# --- 4) Stack them and add a single title --------------------------------
Plot_Protein_Abundance_Boxplot_Per_Date <- (p1 / p2 / p3) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Protein Abundance of deteceted Protein per Date", subtitle = subtitle, theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
                                                                                                                plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5)))



# Save as A4 PDFs
ggsave("Plot_Protein_Signal_Overview.png",
       plot   = Plot_Protein_Abundance_Boxplot_Per_Date,
       width  = 11.69, 
       height =  8.27, 
       units = "in", 
       device = "png")


```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# PCA of metaproteomes by month
# - log2-transform protein abundances
# - reshape to sample x protein matrix
# - run PCA
# - draw statistical ellipses for months with >= 4 samples
# - draw a manual enclosing ellipse for very small groups (e.g. June)
# - place month labels at centroid-based offset positions
# - use colors from the already loaded ColorPalette
# ============================================================

# Load libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(ggforce)
library(grid)   # for unit()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Filter and log-transform protein abundance
# ------------------------------------------------------------
protein_df <- dat %>%
  select(Date, Protein_ID, Protein_Abundance_IBAQ) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  filter(!is.na(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ), Protein_Abundance_IBAQ > 0) %>%
  mutate(log_ibaq = log2(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Pivot to wide format: one row per date, one column per protein
# ------------------------------------------------------------
wide_protein_df <- protein_df %>%
  select(Date, Protein_ID, log_ibaq) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = Protein_ID,
    values_from = log_ibaq,
    values_fill = list(log_ibaq = 0)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Date)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) PCA
# ------------------------------------------------------------
pca_input <- as.data.frame(wide_protein_df)
rownames(pca_input) <- as.character(pca_input$Date)
pca_input <- pca_input[, -1, drop = FALSE]

pca_result <- prcomp(pca_input, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Variance explained
# ------------------------------------------------------------
pca_var <- pca_result$sdev^2
pca_var_exp <- round(100 * pca_var / sum(pca_var), 1)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Prepare PCA dataframe
# ------------------------------------------------------------
pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca_result$x)
pca_df$Date <- as.Date(rownames(pca_df))
pca_df$Month <- factor(
  month(pca_df$Date, label = TRUE, abbr = TRUE),
  levels = c("Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun")
)

# keep only relevant months explicitly
pca_df <- pca_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Month))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Define month colors from loaded ColorPalette
#    Adjust indices if you prefer other colors from your palette
# ------------------------------------------------------------
month_cols <- c(
  Mar = ColorPalette[3],
  Apr = ColorPalette[2],
  May = ColorPalette[5],
  Jun = ColorPalette[7]
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Split groups by size for ellipse drawing
# ------------------------------------------------------------
ellipse_df <- pca_df %>%
  group_by(Month) %>%
  mutate(n_group = n()) %>%
  ungroup()

enough_points <- ellipse_df %>% filter(n_group >= 4)
few_points    <- ellipse_df %>% filter(n_group < 4)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 8) Compute centroid-based month label positions
#    Offsets are chosen to move labels away from dense point clouds
# ------------------------------------------------------------
label_df <- pca_df %>%
  group_by(Month) %>%
  summarise(
    PC1 = mean(PC1, na.rm = TRUE),
    PC2 = mean(PC2, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    PC1 = PC1 + case_when(
      Month == "Mar" ~ -18,
      Month == "Apr" ~   8,
      Month == "May" ~  18,
      Month == "Jun" ~  -6,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    PC2 = PC2 + case_when(
      Month == "Mar" ~  18,
      Month == "Apr" ~  28,
      Month == "May" ~ -18,
      Month == "Jun" ~   8,
      TRUE ~ 0
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 9) Build plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------
pca_plot <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Month)) +

  # Statistical ellipses for groups with enough points
  stat_ellipse(
    data = enough_points,
    aes(fill = Month, group = Month),
    geom = "polygon",
    type = "norm",
    level = 0.95,
    alpha = 0.12,
    linewidth = 1,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  # Manual enclosing ellipse for groups with too few points
  geom_mark_ellipse(
    data = few_points,
    aes(fill = Month, group = Month),
    alpha = 0.12,
    expand = unit(2, "mm"),
    con.cap = 0,
    label.fontsize = 0,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  # Sample points
  geom_point(size = 3) +

  # Date labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Date),
    vjust = -0.7,
    size = 3,
    check_overlap = TRUE,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  # Month labels
  geom_text(
    data = label_df,
    aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, label = Month),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = month_cols[as.character(label_df$Month)],
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 6,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  # Manual colors from ColorPalette
  scale_color_manual(values = month_cols) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = month_cols) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA of Protein Abundances by Month",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", pca_var_exp[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", pca_var_exp[2], "%)")
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Show plot
pca_plot

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 10) Save plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = "PCA_metaproteomes_by_month.png",
  plot = pca_plot,
  width = 14,
  height = 9,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 300
)
```
